/cwe/weakness/663.Name: Use of a Non-reentrant Function in a Concurrent Context
/cwe/weakness/663.Description: The product calls a non-reentrant function in a concurrent context in which a competing code sequence (e.g. thread or signal handler) may have an opportunity to call the same function or otherwise influence its state.

Entries.IntroText: In this example, a signal handler uses syslog() to log a message:
The following code relies on getlogin() to determine whether or not a user is trusted. It is easily subverted.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Add synchronization to your non-reentrant function.
In Java, use the ReentrantLock Class.
